label: 143_netanyahu_likud_herzog_knesset
description: The Israeli elections saw Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's Likud party leading with strong support from voters. This followed months of political instability, including a government without a budget and constitutional crises between the parliament and supreme court.

earliest_articles.title: Israel Heads to Elections
earliest_articles.excerpt: Ending rampant speculation here in Israel, Prime Minister Binyamin Netanyahu today announced his intentions to hold early elections–likely in January or early February–setting the stage for a short campaign in which Netanyahu is the clear front-runner. A Battle to Set the Agenda Israel’s political parties now prepare…

earliest_articles.title: Bibi Can’t Lose in the Upcoming Israeli Elections
earliest_articles.excerpt: It’s one of Washington’s worst kept secrets: President Barack Obama’s administration would prefer Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to lose the Israeli elections in January 2013. Netanyahu is not only too hawkish on the Palestinian issue and Iran for the White House’s comfort, he has the added burden of a fraught…

earliest_articles.title: Israel Elections: Netanyahu’s New Coalition Troubles
earliest_articles.excerpt: Update: Exit polls indeed suggest a very narrow victory for Netanyahu’s right wing/religious bloc. The results suggest a difficult task ahead for Netanyahu of building—and maintaining—a stable coalition. Israelis head to the polls today—January 22—to elect the 19th Knesset, Israel’s parliament. newest_articles.title: What Benny Gantz’s resignation means for Israeli policy and politics
newest_articles.excerpt: On Sunday, June 9, Israeli minister Benny Gantz, a member of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s war cabinet and Netanyahu’s main putative challenger for the position of prime minister, resigned from the government along with his fellow party member Gadi Eisenkot. The resignation comes at an awkward time for…

central_articles.title: In Israel, Benny Gantz decides to join with rival Netanyahu
central_articles.excerpt: After three national elections, a worldwide pandemic, months of a government operating with no new budget, a prime minister indicted in three criminal cases, and a genuine constitutional crisis between the parliament and the supreme court, Israel has landed bruised and damaged where it could have been a year ago. central_articles.title: Israeli Elections: Who Will Win? (The Numbers, Take 1)
central_articles.excerpt: Editor’s note: Israelis will go to the polls on March 17 to elect the 20th Knesset, Israel’s parliament, which will then form a new government to replace Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s current one (he hopes to head the next government as well.) We are following the run-up to the elections in a series here on…

central_articles.title: How Bibi pulled it off
central_articles.excerpt: The Israeli elections — an unofficial referendum on the tenure of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu — ended Tuesday in a clear endorsement of the prime minister by Israeli voters. With over 99 percent of the votes counted, Netanyahu’s Likud leads with 29 or possibly 30 seats in the 120-seat Knesset, compared…
